Title: After being a customer for over 10…
Content: After being a customer for over 10 years i had to change my bank details. They didn't update this on my file and so my payment lapsed, instead of informing me then cancelled my policy. When i tried to fix it they told me i'd have to take out a new policy and refused to help me further. When i raised a complaint they told me it was my fault they hadn't updated my details even though the representative had record of my changing them and had both the old and new bank details on file. They refuse to take responsibility for the mistake. Do not use this company they are incompetant and cannot handle simple tasks, representatives are outsourced and don't give clear information.

Title: Very Helpful after I called for some advice…

Title: Rubbish
Content: Rubbish! Used to be insured through AXA and for a long time too, my insurance was due and decided to look around and in the meantime the AXA policy expired, tried to get another quote to give them benefit of doubt and as my car is also with them and they said they were unable to quote even though it was insured with them 2 days ago! They then push towards Home Protect  Tried on the site and there is a technical issue sorting the quote out after spending time completing the fields and the chat doesn't work either, not a good start for a new possible customer! Considering they have all the details and their thirst for speaking to people Im surprised that they didn't take to calling as soon as their technical issue was known! They only needed to focus on people getting quotes or making a claim!  If this is the first interaction then it sets a scene! Backed by AXA apparently which means its not AXA so wont effect their name!

Title: Your risk calculator rates everything…
Content: Your risk calculator rates everything as High or Very High.  All this is sleepy Tunbridge Wells.  I especially liked the High rating for flooding when we live on an inclined street miles from any river.  And crime: Very High.  In 30m years of living here: no crime.  Maybe my alarm system helps, but you don't ask about that.

Title: Terrible
Content: Terrible. Where to start, broken in, contacted them, pushed from pillar to post, lost details, confusion, wasted time on the phone.  The claim started in May, As of December not resolved and rejected, reason, we allegedly were not in the property, but were however able to prove financially we were, bills, council tax etc. Underwriter couldn't be bothered to go upstairs to see we were living here.  So, all my Downhill bikes gone, no riding or racing this year, tools etc. Appalling. Stress full times, and all for the princely sum of 140 quid a month.

Title: Nothing short of a rip off
Content: I joined Home Protect in October 2025.  I did ask about a claim, and ARAG who Home Protect assign were actually very helpful.  I then had an unforeseen change in circumstances for work, meaning I needed to cancel the policy as I was moving abroad.  It was explained to me every policy has a built in admin premium of £50, and this would be taken on a pro rata basis.  I understood this to mean that since I'd paid two months of premiums, this should be less than £50, even though a charge of £50, for pushing a button, is in fact daylight robbery, nothing short of commercial greed, which I'm inclined to remember the AXA brand for, having always (until now) found them to transparent.  I've since received an email stating the final premium will be more than the £50, and in fact, circa £30 short of the annual insurance price.  Absolute con.

Content: I wanted to cancel my policy. The website does not provide a phone number. I ended up on live chat in the absence of any other means of contact. Direct speaking is much faster. Direct speaking also means it is possible to identify any misunderstandings at a much earlier stage in the process. I understand there is a charge to use the telephone. Surely not! I'm not sure I would use Homeprotect again if I can only make contact by livechat and have to pay extra for phone calls. I want to be clear I have no criticism of the Homeprotect person, but of the requirement to use live chat.

Title: Shockingly poor treatment – policy voided without notice after months of delay
Content: I have never rated home insurance before but our experience with Homeprotect has been extremely disappointing, and I feel obliged to share it so others understand how this insurer handles concerns and claims. Prior to taking out this policy we had 15 years NCD with house insurance so we are not people who try to claim readily.   Timeline of events and background  Prior to purchase – RICS Level 3 Survey We commissioned a full RICS Level 3 building survey—the most detailed survey available. The survey concluded: The house was typical for its age A minor crack in the damp-proof course was likely due to thermal expansion There were no active structural concerns No further investigation was required before purchase There was no evidence of active subsidence We relied on this expert report to answer Homeprotect’s questions accurately and transparently.  March 2025   Before purchasing the policy, on exchange of contracts with this property, I phoned Homeprotect directly to confirm how we should answer questions relating to historic underpinning. Our property had underpinning 25 years ago, long before our ownership. I explained this clearly, and was told it was not relevant as was outside the 10 year period.   July 2025 – New cracking noticed During a period of unusually hot weather, we noticed some new cracking that appeared to be related to heat and movement in the soil—very common for this summer. We did exactly what a responsible homeowner should: We reported it promptly to Homeprotect. They then appointed their own surveyor.  August 2025 A Homeprotect-appointed surveyor attended the property and we subsequently send over the copy of our pre-purchase survey which they initially claimed to not have received despite email confirmation that it had. We heard nothing further for months. No requests for clarification. No suggestion that cover was at risk. No indication that voidance or cancellation was even being considered.   August–November 2025 – Complete silence We had to put in a complaint to move the process forward.  27th November 2025 – Policy voided immediately, with no notice Out of nowhere, we received a letter stating that our policy had been voided from inception—meaning they were treating it as if it never existed. This letter was the first and only notice. There was no: advance warning opportunity to answer questions opportunity to provide evidence explanation of why this decision was made months after their own surveyor attended  This left us instantly uninsured, which is not only deeply stressful but also contrary to the fair treatment required under FCA regulations. Insurers must give reasonable notice before cancellation; Homprotect gave none.  Why this is so unfair: We acted entirely in good faith: We disclosed historic underpinning before purchasing the policy. We relied on a RICS Level 3 survey showing no active issues. We reported new cracking promptly and transparently. We cooperated fully with Homeprotect’s surveyor. We commissioned an extremely thorough survey which was subsequently used against us - it seems if we had been less diligent and had a basic survey this may not have happened. To void a policy under these circumstances—months later, without notice—appears disproportionate, unjustified, and procedurally improper. It has wide reaching implications for our ability to be able to insure our property in the future, due to no fault of our own.   What happens now We are now forced into a formal complaint process and likely escalation to the Financial Ombudsman. Homeprotect has handled the entire matter with poor communication, no transparency, and an absence of basic fairness and an expectation of a householder that is way beyond what is reasonable.  Final thoughts I would strongly caution anyone considering Homeprotect. Our case has shown that even when you fully disclose everything, commission the highest level of survey, and report issues promptly, you may still find yourself uninsured without warning and left to challenge the decision alone.

Title: Misleading insurance quotes
Content: I insured with Homeprotect last year, and this year looked around as usual for house insurance comparison quotes.  Homeprotect came out with a favourable quote, so I re-insured with them, paid the quoted amount via credit card.  When the credit card bill arrived they'd charged over £91.00 more than I'd paid by credit card.   When challenged, they said that they'd gone by the amount on the autorenewal notice and not the new cheaper quote which I'd found with them - and which I'd paid before the renewal was due.  they then tried to reduce the refund (ie cancel cover) by £142, even though the insurance had only been in place for 3 weeks, stating £50 cancellation for being outside the cooling off period.  However, as I'd only just received the credit card statement (3 wks after I took out the insurance), and realised they'd taken extra money, I had no way of knowing within the 2 week cooling-off period that they'd taken this money without my permission or knowledge.

Title: Off to good start with Home Protect…
Content: This is my first time with Home Protect Insurance, Online Chats during my quotation were informative and quick responses.  The price is competitive for my home which has mainly Flat Roof and also with History of Flood risk.    Regarding roof types, in my experience many online home insurance providers, ( Direct Line, AVIVA, the comparison sites, etc) do not list the modern membranes for covering flat roofs.ie Trocal, PVC, EPDM. I had to contact insurance companies by phone or online Chat to obtain their equivalent type for quotation purposes.  If I selected 'Other' in many cases my application was declined.  My roof  covering  SIKA Trocal, for many was not listed.   In Online Chat with Homeprotect's agent I was instructed to select Fibreglass, and after that completing my details for the quote was straight forward.

Title: HOME INSURANCE CANCELLATION WITHOUT NOTICE TO THE CUSTOMER
Content: Repeated Cancellation Without Notice & Demanding Full Payment Again  I am extremely disappointed with HomeProtect and feel it is important to warn others.  Last year, HomeProtect cancelled our home insurance without any notice, and I was forced to pay the entire annual premium upfront again just to keep our cover in place. I paid it, assuming this was a one-off issue.  However, today I received an email from the Sales Team with an attached letter from the CEO, Mark Eastham, stating that our policy is again cancelled immediately and that our home is no longer protected. The letter provided no explanation, no reason, and no prior warning.  I called HomeProtect straight away and spoke to Sandra. She simply repeated that the policy was cancelled and insisted that if we want to continue cover, we must pay the full annual cost again, as if the previous payment does not exist. When I asked for clarification or justification, she could not explain why the policy was cancelled and appeared not knowledgeable about the company’s own policies or complaints process. The call ended abruptly, and she hung up on me.  So to summarise:  This has now happened twice  No notice given either time  No explanation provided  Told to pay the full premium again  Poor customer service  Complaint handling is unprofessional  This has caused unnecessary stress and has left our home uninsured through no fault of our own.  A home insurance company should offer security, transparency and support — not confusion, cancellation and payment demands.  I strongly advise others to avoid HomeProtect.

Title: Charging £20 penalty fees if the monthly payment doesn't go through
Content: I booked a home insurance plan at £25.73 per month, thinking that I could cancel it any time I wished. However, this is deceiving. After 2 weeks, I received a credit agreement, which means that the plan is annual and they put you to sign this credit agreement. One of my payments failed to get paid automatically by direct debit due to my bank's fault and they immediately started sending me threatening letters, while automatically charging me £20 penalty charge. This is pathetic and despicable. I could have bought insurance elsewhere and pay month by month without any such issues or hefty obligations. I will launch a complaint with the insurance regulator, as this is serious abuse of my consumer's rights.

Title: Just wanted a quote - awful response
Content: My mother-in-law died in March and SAGA allowed her insurance to continue under reduced terms. As we have not sold the flat yet we are looking for "unoccupied" insurance, and Homeprotect came up in a search. So I filled in all my details and was presented with this quote:  For around £300 they will cover: The flat being hit by an Aircraft The flat being struck by lightning or an earthquake A fire or explosion in the unoccupied flat and some public and staff liabilities.  Now, I'm not an actuary but I don't think these are common events.  What they don't cover includes the things I'm looking for in unoccupied flat insurance: Storm or flood Accidental damage Theft Escape of water or oil Aerials & falling objects Malicious damage Frost or weight of snow Subsidence or tree roots Collisions with wild animals or vehicles Damage by emergency services  So almost no cover for £300, how are they still in Business?

Title: Such a waste of time...
Content: I have had homeprotect content insurance for a few years now, I've never had to claim anything and based on my experience trying to add things to my policy I even more hope I never will.  Every time I try and add something it is so frustrating, the customer service clearly doesn't speak enough English to properly use the acros they have so they can't answer basic questions and just repeat previous information.  Today I once again tried to just get a quote of how much the premium will increase, if I add a piece of jewellery . Firstly they can't just give you a basic quote and/or tell you if it's possible to insure an item that's valued at price X, you need to go through all the changes to the basically reject them in the last step. Going through all of these steps, really to just get a quote to see if I would add it to the insurance, or insure it another way took 60min, until I was then told they couldn't tell me and I'd have to call in. Who with a normal job has this much time to waste? Especially, if its then to be followed up with "you need to now call in". I got given a referencenumber, but based on my experience you have to start from zero regardless.  It all ended with me trying to figure out, if I needed to call in by a specific day or time in order for said reference number to work, which my poor customer service person didn't seem to understand so just repeated my reference number and hotline opening times.  Anway, all I want is an insurance that doesn't waste my time and has decent customer service, why is that so impossible to find? Especially considering I'm not even trying to claim anything, but actually trying to add to it aka give them more money.  Anyway, needless to say I will see, if I can find another insurer that can just give me a simple answer on price and conditions and then hopefully get this done without costing me another few hours - recommendations super welcome!

Title: If I could give zero stars
Content: If I could give zero stars, I would. On renewing my policy Homeprotect took it upon themselves to change my direct debit date, attempt to collect 2 direct debit within 2 weeks, try to charge me a late fee for a missed payment they attempted to take on the wrong date of the month... and then attempt to cancel my policy... Customer service has been atrocious! They don't even listen to each other... let alone the customer! I have spent 2 weeks in emotional distress trying to sort this out, and they have still forced me to make my October payment early even after reinstating my policy and agreeing to change my direct debit date. I am currently in the process of making an official complaint for the emotional distress and financial hardship caused. The only good thing that I can say is that their online payment system works much more capably and effectively than their employees.

Title: Sadly disappointed with Homeprotect after a number of years as a policy holder.
Content: I have had a policy with Homeprotect for a number of years now and have previously had no issues or concerns. I have always been treated with respect and kindness when dealing with their staff via phone or online chat. My policy renewal for this coming year, despite my never, ever having made a claim on my insurance, has increased in price by £280.00 to nearly a thousand pounds per year. Surprised by this quite large increase in premium I obviously looked at alternatives and found like for like cover with a number of alternative insurers for just over half the price of my renewal quote and nearly "£200 LESS" than I paid for my cover last year. The very nice man I spoke to at Homeprotect when cancelling my automatic renewal told me that they had experienced an increased number of claims over the last year hence the jump in premiums. He also advised that there is no negotiation or price matching, (thus no loyalty to their customers!). Effectively I, (along with all their other customers), am being asked to cover their losses for last year! We all know that the cost of living increases and our insurances like everything else goes up by an amount each year. However, I as an individual who has never made a claim on any home insurance in 42 years find being asked to ensure that the insurance companies continue to profit, at my cost, leaves a somewhat unpalatable taste in my mouth.

Title: 4 Weeks Without Water
Content: 4 Weeks Without Water – A Heartbreaking Experience with HomeProtect  After 8 years of paying premiums (around £30,000) and never once claiming, our first experience needing help from HomeProtect has been utterly devastating.  A mains water pipe burst under our kitchen 4 weeks ago, and we’ve been living without running water ever since — unable to wash, clean teeth, do laundry, or even flush toilets. Despite the urgency, HomeProtect delayed endlessly, sending multiple assessors but taking no practical action for weeks.  We were repeatedly questioned, accused, and subjected to irrelevant investigations (such as what our oil tank is made from or how many cars we own) — none of which had anything to do with a burst pipe. It felt like they were trying every possible route to avoid accepting liability.  When they finally agreed to repairs, it was only after I escalated directly. Even then, the process was disjointed and chaotic. They’ve now claimed the rebuild costs more than we paid for the entire property including land, forcing us to appoint our own surveyor just to dispute their figures — even though our home’s footprint hasn’t changed since taking the policy.  To add insult to injury, after three weeks of this ordeal, they suggested we stay in a run-down pub with a teenager, five cats, and a dog — a completely unreasonable solution.  HomeProtect proudly advertises record profits, a new AI claims platform, and “enhanced decisioning,” yet their handling of this emergency was inhumane and incompetent. We have all fallen ill during this time due to the unsanitary conditions.  We have now reported the case to the Financial Ombudsman, Watchdog, and The Guardian, who previously ran an article exposing similar issues.  Avoid HomeProtect at all costs.  Their processes are broken, their compassion is nonexistent, and their technology appears to make things worse, not better.   I would love for their leadership team to experience four weeks without water — perhaps then they’d understand what true customer care should look like.

Title: Home insurance company that doesn't pay out
Content: We've recently had some storm damage to our flat roof. Given that I'd taken out a full home and contents policy with Homeprotect I contacted them, only to be told that the damage was caused by wear and tear, not a storm. This is despite a roofing company's confirmation that, as the roof is only 10 years old  and in good repair the damage was consistent with storm damage. What Homeprotect then appear to do is pass you over to one of their associated businesses who recommend that it's not Homeprotect's responsibility, then they send you an email from another company called AGAR mentioning lawyers who can help!  The whole setup is designed to confuse you until you give up. I've never had this type of issue with an insurance company before - the last - and only - time I needed to claim was around 15 years ago on a separate issue with a bona fida well-known insurance company, who's deal - via their easy-to-contact-UK office was "you pay us, if you need us, contact us directly and we'll cover the claim" so - as it turns out - nothing like the Homeprotect policy which seems designed to confuse. I'll be cancelling my policy and moving my business elsewhere. I'm not in the habit of paying £800 pa for such an opaque and questionable service and I would advise caution before committing your insurance to Homeprotect.

Title: Didn't listen to me...
Content: Originally looked on line to find an insurance company for an empty house insurance as our aunt had recently passed. Rang up got a quote for the property and gave my sister in laws address as the person proposing the insurance. All was well until the house sold. My sister in law realised she hadn't signed in for the documents. I rang to get a new email link to be sent. The lady wouldnt do it without speaking to my sister in law. She rang them and then me. Seems the operator on the initial call had insured the property as my regular residence. My sister in law and  I then rang again to get something done about this. We decided the simplest thing was to just cancel as we were aware Homeprotect had at that time not drawn September or Octobers payment only the initial deposit. We were put on hold and then surprise, surprise they had now taken 2 payments and wanted a cancellation fee. So to sum it up cost us over £120.00 for nothing, the ladies on the phone wouldn't listen to us and talked over us while we tried to explain their error. Overall abysmal, if I had wanted to insure the house as a main residence I would have simply continued to use the incumbent Insurer. 0 stars from me, all the blame was laid firmly at our door with absolutely none at their side.

Title: First specialist makes false accusations and then identical claim is approved while ours is rejected
Content: I live in rural area and there are three houses that share the septic tank system which is on my land. The drain field failed and we had to develop a new one. I opened up a claim and it went on to they drainage specialist for evaluation, PCD. I provided them with photos, the timeline and all the receipts and information. The response from PCD was that the cesspit hasn't been emptied regularly and this was the reason with the failure of the drain field.   Everyone living in a rural area knows that cesspit is a system without a drain field and it's merely a tank which needs regular emptying.   So not only PCD either don't know what they are doing which makes them incompetent, or they just made up a reason not to pay for the damage.   After this I explained my handler that PCD has made a mistake as the septic tank had already been emptied in Feb 25 and again Jul 25 after the issues started arising. I also provided them full history of receipts for annual tank emptying.  I was then told that PCD had provided a 'wrong report' to HomeProtect and a new one will be issued. HomeProtect and PCD both use online portals where the documents are uploaded with the reference number, so there's no possibility of 'sending a wrong report' and everything tagged with the claim reference number and my details.   Last Friday I received an email from PDC saying that a 'new report' has been sent to HomeProtect and my handler would get in touch with me.   Today my handler contacted me and told me that my claim was rejected as ' they have advised the claim will need to be declined as the damage was not caused by an insured peril.'  Here's the kicker. In the meanwhile, our neighbour who shares the tank with us, is also with HomeProtect and had their claim already approved and received the compensation.   My claim was reject by HomeProtect's specialist company PDC while 100% identical claim for the same reason on the same property was approved for my neighbour. Now I'm £3000 out of pocket while my neighbour isn't.   To add insult to injury, HomeProtect just sent a renewal notice where the quote is £100 more / 30% higher than last year.   It's two tier justice served by a specialist which doesn't do due diligence and then tries to cover it up.

Title: Avoid at all costs.
Content: Avoid at all costs.  I'm a relatively new customer of theirs, having moved to a new house in April. There was torrential rain, that caused flooding in our house due to rain water backing up through the manhole and into our basement. Just to note, there was over 3ft of water in the house, we had to call the fire department to clear it... it was quite the emergency.  Firstly, they dont have a dedicated emergency line... we were on hold for over 90 minutes, whilst our house had a small swimming pools amount of water in it and growing... no electricity or heating...they tried to claim it was accidental damage, rather than a flood. It's now been almost 2 weeks since this incident, and whilst they've sent out an inspector to assess the damage, they've not done anything else since then despite promising a fast turnaround on getting a 'flood restoration team' out to bring heaters, dehumidfiers etc and to start tearing out the damaged parts of the building. We're now experiencing second order effects such as damp, mould, smells and more.  If you value your home, sanity, wellbeing, I would strongly advise going for a more reputable insurance company.   This outfit are underprepared, will cut at every corner possible, and simply are not there to help you, they're there for themselves only.   Please avoid.

Title: Beware of a 170% hike in premiums
Content: Been with Homeprotect for around 4 years now, and had a fair service. My current premium for last year was (ending mid October 2025) £397  yesterday I got my renewal notice which was on auto renew, for the sum of £1052 an increase of 170% .  I was in shock, I did get an emergency plumber mid year for a leaking pipe which he was here for 45 minutes to repair and although I had emergency cover I still had to pay £90 excess.  I phoned but all I got was no answer, so I used the chat system, the advisor told me prices have gone up but he was shocked to see how much I was being asked to pay, he offered me £80 discount which I told him was a stupid amount looking at how much they wanted to charge for next years premium , he could offer no reason as to why that amount of increase. I cancelled with him and have spoken to my bank to cancel the DD with Homeprotect, I had confirmation by email this morning from the insurers and believe it or not a questionnaire as to why I have cancelled, "HELLO"  I quote from their website and my renewal advice. "When we offer you a renewal price, it will always be the same as, or better or better, than we offer new customers WATCH OUT NEW CUSTOMERS.   Unexpected call this morning from yourselves, however after discussion and the final outcome I personally find that the company and person representing the company to be "Two Faced" in his dealings and added to that totally weak in his approach whilst on the phone and the final findings as outlined. In conclusion my findings are that this ( Homeprotect ) is a company that cannot be trusted and I hope that anyone considering them treads carefully before parting with any monies.

Title: Probably the most difficult company to contact
Content: Absolute nightmare trying to get hold of them. I always shop around for quotes for my home insurance. I had been with these and had not received my renewal notice, that’s where all of the problems started. The telephone number they give you is one of these automated ones and I found it impossible to speak to anyone. There is no email address, if you use one that they have sent to you, all you get to that is ‘this address is not monitored.’ When I tried to access their web site all I got was ‘does not exist although I believe that has now changed but it is still a nightmare trying to contact them. As my policy was on auto renewal as they could not be contacted the only way I could ensure no money was taken, because at that time I had no idea what the renewal premium was, was to contact my bank and ask them to ensure payments were not made. If an insurance company is that difficult to contact, I have no wish to use them

Title: Misleading Charges and Unexplained Charges
Content: Title: Misleading Policy & Unexplained Charges  I took out building insurance with Homeprotect because they were the only company I could find that would insure an empty house. From the start, I made it clear that the property was up for sale and the policy would be cancelled upon completion. For this reason, I chose to pay monthly instalments.  I specifically asked if there were any cancellation fees and was told “no.” Payments were £21.95 per month. When the house sold, I cancelled the policy as agreed. Shortly after, I received an email stating I owed £85.98, in addition on the same day Homeprotect still took the regular £21.95 from my account.  That means they believe I now owe them £107.93 — and I cannot understand why.  I contacted them, but the call handler could only say a “case has been raised with the accounts department.” I am now left waiting for answers.  Until this is resolved, I am warning others: be very cautious. Based on my experience — and the large number of other 1-star reviews here — I would avoid this company. If Homeprotect do resolve my case fairly, I will be more than happy to update or remove this review.  UPDATE  First of all thank you to the Homeprotect team for raising a complaint on the back of this Trustpilot review. The Monday after I received a phone call from Macauley a member of the customer complaints team. He was very polite and professional. He had taken the time to review and investigate my complaint. I won’t go into any details but I was informed that unfortunately I was badly advised by one of Homeprotect’s handling team. As a result I will not have to pay the £85.98 and will be refunded part of the £21.95.  In summary a big thank you to Homeprotect and in particular Macauley. Mistakes can be made but if mistakes are quickly rectified then I am satisfied. I could have deleted this review, but preferred to update so any potential Homeprotect customers can see that Homeprotect can act quickly and professionally if there is a problem.

Title: Very threatening letter
Content: They sent me a very threatening default letter because I missed a payment - instead of collecting it a few days later as normal for most companies when something goes wrong.    I don't know why this happened. Perhaps my card was out of date.    The email was completely strange.  I had signed the agreement months ago but it  read:  If you're yet to register and/or sign your Credit Agreement, please follow the link below, to register you'll need your;  Agreement Reference (***) Correspondence postcode; Surname; and, Date of birth All of this was not relevant so I ignored it.    But the actual important letter was a pdf attachment which I did not read.     Why not communicate something in the email that was relevant?  Then I got the  letter in the post. Pretty horrible threatening letter.   They charged me £20 for missed collection.    Then it was incredibly hard to find link to make payment. I don't normally bother to look around at renewal time, but next time I will. I'm really annoyed.  Why treatment customers like criminals?  Why not learn some basic communication skills?

Title: Everything went wrong
Content: Everything went wrong.  Don't touch them - useless if you actually have to claim.  Had an escape of water and was fully insured for both emergency and all the extras.  the call centre that was to arrange accommodation said I was not covered - I was.  They said they would arrange accommodation via a third party - who were unable to help so put it out to a 4th party who didn't act in a timely manner and my disabled son and carer had no accommodation.  It was appalling.  I made a complaint and absolutely nothing happened.  They totally don't care.   The call centre refused to provide temporary heaters - said |I was not eligible and it was not possible - I was eligible and it was possible and they eventually approved payment eventually. Every single step was a battle and traumatic.   Some many many months later (I was advised nothing would even start for 3 months because that's normal processing time) when the claim was resolved the insurer said they would pay in 7-10 days but they repeatedly said this three different times and consequently my cash flow was very negatively impacted as I had unfortunately believe them.  I absolutely woudl not touch this company unless you are confident you will never have to claim.  The customer experience was absolutely appalling.

Title: Awful experience
Content: Awful experience, took out a policy to cover us whilst we were having structural work. HomeProtect seemed like a good option as they would cover us for this, we took out a monthly policy and intended to cancel as soon as the structural work was complete as the policy was extremely expensive in comparison to a normal house insurance policy.   We had nothing but trouble trying to cancel the policy. We cancelled our direct debit to try and trigger a cancellation as you can only cancel your policy by chatting with them or ringing them when their lines are open. Unfortunately due to use both working full time we have been unable to cancel the policy due easily because of the issue of their lines closing before we arrive back from work. We have now been charged a £50 administration fee for cancelling our policy after finally managing to get through, though this was not made clear to us when we took out the policy. Would avoid HomeProtect at all costs on this basis unfortunately!

Title: Terms & Conditions !
Content: I like Homeprotect, they have dealt with all my past problems well and with intelligence. As with any intangible product there are limits and it's vital to know how far your cover will go in helping with an emergency. My whole saga involves Homeprotect and British Gas and ran from April till last week - at no time did anyone breach a contract or fail to follow laid - down procedure  BUT  ...... I of all people ought to have known what the contracts say: Always READ the info! - Brit. Gas can only deal with bits of plumbing thaty are part of your current system (boilers, controls, hot water and radiators) - old bits of pipe are not their business.  - There are two sorts of gas engineer - "gas" and "wet".  - Homeprotect Emergency plumbers are quick, efficient but are only able to turn off at the mains and repair a leak that's visible. They are brilliant at suggesting the best area of ceiling likely to be below the leak, which could be buried in a brick wall... - Track & Trace are closed at weekends.  - If it's raining in the kitchen you need an umbrella. - If you think you've been clever sawing out the ceiling and fixing the problem, you may just have delayed the inevitable catastrophic failure by (say) 3 months. You are not likely to have the experience to know better.  Issue now 90% eliminated thanks to my ability to solder joints, fit compression fittings / stop valves and do tiling to Flintstones standards.   All plasterers are busy till October .... it's just something they are, get over it.  You would be crazy not to cover your house - just be aware tht ano policy covers every imaginable disaster (let alone the unimaginable ones)

Title: Homeprotect offer specialised home and…

Having said that, Homeprotect was extremely easy to deal with and I was delighted to find an insurer to cover my specific needs at all!  They were professional and knowledgeable and I felt reassured that they understood my requirements.  Their paperwork was delivered by email and everything ran very smoothly. The proof of any insurance is of course only really clear at the point a claim has to be made, an event that I am very much hoping to avoid. But if their claims process is as good as their set-up procedures, I am sure I will be a very happy customer!

Content: When I changed my policy from vacant executor's insurance to second home I had an appalling experience with customer service, an extremely long almost unintelligible call on a poor line. I then wanted to put in a complaint and this took ages with the agent saying he could not email to confirm I had made a complaint. Again after insisting I did receive an email but with no details of my complaint. All this took over an hour. I dread to think what it would be like to make a claim from them. Any dealings are a huge struggle. My advice is don't go there.

Title: ** DON'T use Homeprotect to "protect"…
Content: ** DON'T use Homeprotect to "protect" your home ** When you raise claim, they provide false information and changed information in their website in order to mislead you and refused the claim. Their staff in different sections provided different false statements to mislead you including: 1)  they said "wording documents" does not show your policy number. In fact, the wording document is a booklet to explain the policy; I was told by another insurance company, the booklet normally will not have your policy number.  2) they ask you to check the information again AFTER  changing their website information; 3)  they said you bought the insurance online by YOURSELF, you have obligation to make sure you bought correct policy despite you have immediately discussed with their staff through WEB CHAT and has CONFIRMED that you bought a correct insurance.  They DENIED everything they said and provided false information to you. Most staff handled my case were non-British names (I may be wrong),  like the last one who contacted us (on 14 Jul 2025) was Z"""D""" who is Customer Relations Officer. I have EXPRESSLY told them that the incident had been STOPPED but it happened again. I repeatedly told their legal team to ignore previous incidents unless they want to deal with it. However, Ms Z"""D""" said the incident was started from 2012 which the policy could not cover the claim.      My understanding is ..... if your property was damaged before and has repaired. After your purchased an insurance from Homeprotect, your property is damaged again, then the Homeprotect will not accept your claim.   If you insist to use this company, you MUST copy each answer you provide in their online form. They may change your answers when you make a claim.   I requested them to refund if they refuse my claim, they said they only refund £100 to me, but I paid £329 for the insurance. One month passed, I have not received the money.   I am going to raise a complaint to the Ombudsman against them for carrying out fraudulent act to deceive their clients.

Content: My home insurance renewal arrived and had gone up by 30%. I shopped around and got a cheaper quote, I phoned Homeprotect to see if they could match or get near to my new quote to be told by a guy that there’s wasn’t anything he could do, I asked what about customer loyalty and he said there isn’t any!!! I was gobsmacked. I asked if he could try and match my new quote for him to say “without changing your policy there’s nothing he could do” he never offered to look into my policy to see if we could make changes. I said I’d have to go elsewhere but was disappointed after being a loyal customer and he said “fine” I phoned Homeprotect the next day as I had a feeling he hadn’t cancelled my policy and low and behold he hadn’t!! The girl I spoke to was very helpful and apologetic for her colleague’s attitude towards me.  (My home insurance has gone up by 80% in the past two years with this company I haven’t once made a claim in since being with this company for many years.) the total lack of customer service is shocking.

So I can't speak for their insurance, but I can say that if you like receiving multiple spammy marketing emails, home protect are the ones to go for.  (EDIT) - I see that "We’ve detected that this company may be asking for reviews in a way that Trustpilot doesn’t support. This can lead to bias and compromise the reliability of reviews." - so it's the full monty from them.  (UPDATE 31 July) I am not going to send my policy ref number and all those details through trustpilot.  I sent multiple emails to homeprotect customer services, just reply to them.  again - what a major red flag this is. Reply to  my mails and contact with your own customer services/ my account rather than this 3rd party please. I have been with you 1 day, and sent emails to your CEO, marketing, Data protection and general customer services teams. If those don't get escalated, what does?

Mums house was empty and she was in dementia care since 2022,  I notified Home Protect Home insurance that the property was sold in Jun2025 and cancelled the Direct Debit. I checked mums bank last night and Home Protect have taken over £46 that was unauthorised and because the direct debit was cancelled the staff must have looked for the first payment that was taken in 2023 so totally disgusting that a ln insurance company could be so ruthless with a vulnerable old lady who has had her home sold to pay for her care fees. I complained and will take this to the FOS.

Title: HomeProtect offer cheaper deals to new customers
Content: I have been with HomeProtect for a couple of years and they promise that they will not offer new customers cheaper insurance.   This is not the case as I put in exactly same details on one of the search engines and HomeProtect offered same insurance at £ 50.00 cheaper when house insurance due in May 25.   I contacted customer services who promised to refund the £ 50 but said this would probably be in 60 days time.   I received £ 25 from them not the £ 50.   I have emailed them twice but no response.  Clearly they do offer better terms to new customers and I will look elsewhere next year.  Following this review HomeProtect have been in touch and refunded the £25 and also an extra £ 50 for goodwill.    Nice customer services touch - but they should have responded to my emails and not offered a cheaper price online.

Title: Dissapointing emergency cover
Content: I called to make a claim on my emergency cover due to a leaking hot water pipe under the kitchen sink. At 4.30 pm, I was told I could a expect a call in an hour to confirm the appointment time. At 6.20 pm I was offered an appointment for the next day. I would expect a much quicker response for 'emergency cover'. I explained  what the problem was three times and asked if I should arrange someone myself or if they would cover replacing the pipe as part of the policy, I was told they "aim to fix during the visit" which led me to believe I could rely on them. Then at 3.30 on a Friday their engineer has been and done nothing (when a 5 minute job would do it) , saying it wasn't covered and I needed to get a plumber. I didn't even have the option of paying them to do the job. I had made it very clear the pipe needed replacing and offered to arrange it independently, instead, over 24 hours with no hot water was wasted, only for nothing to be done, and I now have to pay a premium to get an actual emergency plumber out, when I could have had this done already in normal work hours today if I hadn't realised lied on Home protect. Very dissapointed in this experience.

Content: For an insurance company that is providing cover for your home, trust and competency are key, as well as competitiveness.   Having been a  satisfied repeat customer with Homeprotect,  I recently tried to renew my insurance. Que stress and trust decimation.  1) The renewal price was above that for what they were quoting on cost comparison website. The reason given were minor details , which I updated with them ,and a "claim" in 2023. We enquired back then if something was covered but did not to my knowledge ever make a claim. I could not be told what this claim was as it was 'with the claims team'. I have requested full information and clarification as to what reasonable steps they took to inform me that a call for info could affect premiums. No reply despite the agent agreeing to look into it.   2) The next day when attempting to pay the renewal, the cost had gone up by about £100 because " the previous operator had pressed a button". They said the would honour the quote but after a complex process with accounts. The operator repeatedly changed the story of what was happening. Talk about building confidence!   3) With trust mostly gone but payment processing, I requested a written summary of how the refund and cashback would be processed and by when, including who would be chasing it up on my behalf. This was apparently a step too far from what was possible. I suggested they try harder.  I'm still waiting for this confirmation and for details from their claims team. I understand that insurance companies play a game to be competitive, but clear communication, effective systems, and an iota of competence surely are still useful for their business model.

Title: AVOID
Content: Be very careful! This company took £650+ for buildings insurance. 11 months later, I called to report a minor issue (not a claim) and I was told I did not exist on their database. I gave my policy number but was still told there was no record of me having insurance with this company. The call handler logged a formal complaint and I said I would need to get insurance urgently elsewhere. Whereupon I was told there would be a cancellation fee for the non- existent insurance! To rub salt in the wound they repeatedly tried to take money from my account to renew! Renew what? Needless to say, I rate this company despicable and I took the case to the ombudsman. In any other circumstances, if you buy something and don't receive it, you get your money back but not it seems in the case of insurance. Somehow, I've been fobbed off with 'The company probably would have had to honour a claim'. So £650+ to 'probably' be covered. If I had money I would drag this company through the courts which is exactly what it deserves. They tried to give me £50 to shut me up and the ombudsman has stipulated this be increased to £200 compensation. To date, I still don't know where the £650+ went and who benefitted from it but for 11 months my property wasn't insured but it 'may' have been covered in an emergency. In short Home Protect DON'T protect. AVOID-1 star is the lowest I could give and don't rely on the ombudsman either. I have reported this company to the FCA. Update- I've been asked to supply more details -policy number! As I don't exist on their database I'm not sure how useful that will be. Hopefully, they'll have a record of my complaint. I would welcome information as to what happened to my £650+ that was taken but wasn't used to insure my property.

Title: The very best quote for an unoccupied property and very speedy response to online chat queries
Content: The price for an unoccupied property going through probate was unbeatable. It was irritating to find the days unoccupied defaulted to being 0-30 whilst the property status showed as unoccupied when the documents were produced, but chatting online resolved that to being up to 365 days without any extra charges.  We have had empty property insurance in the past, the conditions abd cover on this policy match what most other companies offer but for a fraction of the price.  With all insurance, the real test is if a claim needs to be made, so I hope (like most of us do) that I never need to find out how good that process is,  if that ever occurs I will share that experience.

Title: Probate house insurance
Content: I purchased temporary insurance to cover my deceased mothers empty home pending probate and sale (so it was clear it was expected to be a short term policy). Not the easiest of times emotionally, so you rely to a large extent on those you are dealing with to be reasonably open and straight forward (it was advertised as probate / empty house insurance). It appears in the small print of the policy there is a catch that means the pro-rata refund you were expecting/led to believe would occur is actually heavily impacted by admin fees when you cancel and that some policy 'extras' (that you thought might be needed given probate is involved and its an empty property) are actually non-refundable. In short, a 12 month policy that was in place for 3 months resulted in a refund of just 36% of the original payment - a great return for Home Protect given I only 'used' 25% of the policy and was expecting a 75% refund! Buyer beware, if you purchase a policy to cover a probate/empty house situation early cancellation will come at a significant cost...

Title: Avoid Homeprotect – Misleading, Unfair, and Borderline Fraudulent
Content: I strongly advise anyone considering Homeprotect to look elsewhere. I submitted a claim for a monitor that was accidentally knocked off my desk by a gust of wind, a clear case of accidental damage. Despite my policy explicitly covering Full Contents Accidental Damage, Homeprotect rejected the claim by falsely classifying it as storm damage and then claimed the wind was not strong enough to qualify.  This is classic bait and switch insurance handling: sell policies promising comprehensive cover, then refuse to pay out by applying technicalities that have nothing to do with the actual incident. It is deliberately misleading and feels like fraud by misrepresentation.  I have sought legal advice and escalated my case. I am also taking it to the Financial Ombudsman and warning others: Homeprotect will happily take your premiums but will fight tooth and nail to deny even the most valid claim.  Their customer service is robotic, evasive, and completely uninterested in fair outcomes. You are just a policy number to them.  Avoid Homeprotect at all costs. There are better and more honest insurers out there
